articles.title: Stock Market Live Updates: Gift Nifty signals lackluster start; Asian stocks bounce back - The Economic Times
articles.description: Sensex Today | Nifty 50 | Stock Market Live Updates | Indian benchmark indices came under heavy selling pressure on 8 June, weighed down by weak global cues, rising geopolitical tensions, and a sharp jump in crude oil prices. The spike in oil prices has heightened concerns about inflation and its potential drag on economic growth.

articles.description: Petrol, Diesel, CNG Prices on June 9 2026 stay unchanged, LPG and CNG see recent hikes, Hyderabad and Thiruvananthapuram record the highest fuel rates

articles.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints muted opening; US-Iran war, gold to crude oil prices - eight stocks to buy or sell
articles.description: Indian stock markets are expected to open flat-to-negative on June 9, 2026, with Gift Nifty at 23,121.5. This follows a significant drop on Monday due to geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices, amid cautious global sentiment and increasing market volatility.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel prices on June 9: Check fuel cost in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Kolkata and other cities today
articles.description: Fuel prices were unchanged today on Tuesday, 9 June. The last fuel price hike was on Monday, 25 May, when oil marketing companies (OMCs) hiked both petrol and diesel prices by more than ₹2.50 per litre.

articles.title: Trump approval near record low as Iran war and gas prices drive voter discontent
articles.description: US President Donald Trump's approval rating remains near a career low, a Reuters/Ipsos poll shows, as most Americans expect gas prices to rise and express growing concern over living costs.

articles.title: Trump's approval stays near record low as Iran war fuels economic concerns
articles.description: The Republican leader has faced widespread discontent in recent months over ​his decision to go to war with Iran, which has led to a surge in gasoline prices

articles.title: D-Street indices caught in crossfire as oil roads lead to uncertainty
articles.description: Indian stock markets tumbled nearly 1% on Monday, mirroring a broader Asian decline. Renewed Middle East hostilities fueled oil prices, while global investors shifted away from AI stocks. The Nifty closed down 243.7 points at 23,123, and the Sensex fell 719.08 points to 73,524.26, reflecting heightened market anxiety.

articles.title: 'Oil Prices Will Come Tumbling Down': Trump Says US Will Declare 'Total Victory' Over Iran In Next Two Weeks
articles.description: The remarks came as Iran and Israel appeared to halt a fresh exchange of missile strikes that had flared over the weekend.
